| 1 | @Iterate | Bootstrapped iteration packets | run-ledger.md | Selected .github/plans/hot-path-performance-observability-1-baseline-runtime.md on main at 029ea6bb4b870522758cf83903dfdfb8eadeec8d. |

| 4 | @Inscribe | Prepared initial implementation commit | commit-packet.md | Created feature/hot-path-performance-observability-1-baseline-runtime from main, recorded the single-commit scope, and preserved the verification blocker that the rebuilt baseline had many 503s/timeouts, 0 acquire successes, and 0 releases. |
| 5 | @Index | Recorded blocked-stop transition before review | execution-report.md | On feature/hot-path-performance-observability-1-baseline-runtime at b0958b9, the rebuilt artifact .github/plans/hot-path-performance-baseline-before.json exists but is not clean: runtime summary has 685 service-unavailable responses, acquire successes were 0, release count was 0, direct StorageApi access-check took about 8084.8 ms, and public API access-check returned 503 after about 5043.2 ms. Resume from blocker triage before review. |
| 6 | @Inscribe | Prepared blocked closeout bookkeeping commit | commit-packet.md | Stayed on feature/hot-path-performance-observability-1-baseline-runtime and staged only agent-authored packet/report/progress-note updates. The closeout commit hash and push result are reported in @Inscribe's final response to avoid a self-referential dirty-file loop. |
| 7 | @Index | Recorded user reopen decision after blocked stop | decision-log.md | User clarified that many 503s are part of the problem later plan steps are meant to resolve and should not stop Step 1 by themselves. If the rebuilt before artifact is too degraded for speedup comparison, @Implement should use the provisional artifact as the before anchor, including copying provisional data to the before artifact. Current branch is feature/hot-path-performance-observability-1-baseline-runtime; latest known commits are b0958b9 initial implementation and 28022b8 blocked closeout bookkeeping. Next consumer is @Implement. |
| 8 | @Implement | Applied DEC-001 baseline anchor follow-up | implementation-handoff.md | Replaced .github/plans/hot-path-performance-baseline-before.json with the provisional baseline data because the rebuilt source-run artifact was too degraded for speedup comparison. Preserved degraded evidence in the handoff: runtime 694 requests, 685 service-unavailable responses, acquire successes 0, release count 0, direct StorageApi access-check about 8084.8 ms, and public API access-check 503 after about 5043.2 ms. Cheap JSON parse/data-identity/encoding checks and diff hygiene passed; full 60s benchmark was not rerun. |
| 9 | @Inscribe | Prepared user-decision follow-up commit | commit-packet.md | Stayed on feature/hot-path-performance-observability-1-baseline-runtime and staged only the DEC-001 before artifact replacement plus related iteration/progress packet updates. Commit hash and push result are reported in @Inscribe's final response to avoid a self-referential dirty-file loop. |

| 12 | @Index | Remediated RVW-001 progress note state | .github/agent-progress/hot-path-performance-observability-1-baseline-runtime.md | Updated the progress note to say DEC-001 follow-up is committed as d6099de, the before artifact now uses provisional baseline data as the comparison anchor, degraded rebuilt-source evidence is preserved only as context, and the loop is ready for re-review/intake after the RVW-001 bookkeeping commit. |
